free_text: Cross section measurements for the (3He,p) nuclear
reaction on B and N between 2 and 4 MeV
Energies calculated for center of target.
Silicon surface-barrier detector covered with
Mylar foil to stop elastically scattered 3He+ ions.
Data received by email, L. McIntyre, 25 September 1998
Approved by L. McIntyre, 6 Nov 1998.
2.6x10**18 atoms/cm2 boron on Cu backing.
Sample 45 keV thick at 3 MeV.
Absolute scale set relative to arial density measured
for a self supporting natural B foil, assuming
Rutherford scattering cross section at 1300 keV.
Corrected for isotopic abundance (19.9%).
Absolute uncertainty due mainly to
uncertainty in reference areal density and solid
angle.
Statistical uncertainty 2-5%.

2.6x10**18 atoms/cm2 boron on Cu backing.
Sample 45 keV thick at 3 MeV.
Absolute scale set relative to arial density measured
for a self supporting natural B foil, assuming

Corrected for isotopic abundance (80.1%).
Absolute uncertainty due mainly to
uncertainty in reference areal density and solid
angle.
Statistical uncertainty 2-5%.
TaSiN on Si target containing 5.5x10**17 atoms/cm2 of
nitrogen.
TaSiN target is 40 keV thick at 3 MeV.
Absolute scale set relative to arial density measured
for a SiN on carbon target.
Absolute uncertainty due mainly to
uncertainty in reference areal density and solid
angle.
Statistical uncertainty 2-5%.
